edible_parts: Seeds (piñon nuts — rich, raw or roasted).
other_uses: The classic Southwestern piñon pine, source of the beloved piñon nut.
prep: Roast and shell the fat, buttery seeds — a premier Southwestern nut food, gathered by the sackful and central to Puebloan and other cuisines. A slow-growing high-desert tree.
harvest: Nuts fall (good crops every few years).
soil: Dry, rocky, well-drained soils; high desert.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
